# Great Swamp Bonsai Society (GSBS)
# https://www.greatswampbonsai.com

> Great Swamp Bonsai Society is New Jersey's premier bonsai club, founded in 1976 and named after the Great Swamp National Wildlife Refuge in Morris County, NJ. We hold free monthly meetings on the 2nd Tuesday of every month (6:00-9:00 PM, no December meeting) at the Essex County Environmental Center, 621-B Eagle Rock Ave, Roseland, NJ 07068. Beginners are welcome — no experience or tree required. Annual dues are $40/year. We are celebrating our 50th anniversary in 2026.

## Key Facts

- **Founded:** 1976 (50th anniversary in 2026)
- **Location:** Essex County Environmental Center, 621-B Eagle Rock Ave, Roseland, NJ 07068
- **Meetings:** 2nd Tuesday of every month, 6:00-9:00 PM (no December meeting)
- **Cost:** FREE to attend. Annual membership dues: $40/year
- **Age:** Adults and children ages 12+
- **President:** Diego Garnica
- **Email:** great.swampbonsai@gmail.com
- **Artist in Residence:** Sergio Cuan (2x Emmy Award winner, former Nickelodeon SVP)
- **Potter in Residence:** Fred Aufschlager (author, "Bonsai Containers as Ceramic Art")
- **Affiliations:** Mid-Atlantic Bonsai Societies (MABS), NJ Bonsai Societies (NJBS)
- **Members:** From eight NJ counties and Eastern Pennsylvania

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/): Overview, next meeting info, upcoming events, location map
- [About](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/about): Club history since 1976, officers, artist/potter in residence, guest artists, member accomplishments, affiliations
- [Meetings](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/meetings): Schedule, location, directions, what to bring, first visit info
- [Events](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/events): 2026 calendar including Annual Bonsai Show (June 6), Jonas Dupuich workshop (April 14), MABS Festival, community events
- [Gallery](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/gallery): 608 photos of member trees, annual show exhibits (2004-2011), MABS Spring Festival exhibits (2005, 2006, 2010, 2011, 2012), Boon Manakitivipart 2011 workshop, step-by-step guest artist demo sequences, workshops, meetings — filterable by category and year
- [Membership](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/membership): How to join, benefits, FAQ
- [Resources](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/resources): Bonsai supplies, learning resources, monthly care guide, newsletter archive (2005-2022, 170+ issues), historical documents, external links
- [Contact](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/contact): Contact form, map, social links

## Upcoming Events (2026)

- **April 14:** Jonas Dupuich Workshop (~$50, BYOT) and evening lecture/demo
- **April 17-19:** MABS Spring Festival, Grantville PA
- **April 19:** Essex County Cherry Blossom Festival, Branch Brook Park
- **April 25:** Essex County Earth Day booth
- **May 12:** Monthly Meeting — air layering and propagation
- **June 6:** Annual Bonsai Show, Garibaldi Hall, 11 AM-3 PM, FREE
- **June 24-29:** Longwood Gardens Bonsai Exhibition excursion

## Pages (Expanded)

- [History](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/history): Detailed timeline with 50+ events from founding to present, notable members, presidential history, memorials
- [Care Guide](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/care-guide): Interactive month-by-month bonsai care guide with species-specific tips for deciduous, coniferous, and tropical trees
- [Beginners](https://www.greatswampbonsai.com/beginners): First tree recommendations, essential tools, common mistakes, what to expect at meetings

## Guest Artists (42+ since 2003, including Budi Sulistyo from Indonesia)

Marco Invernizzi (Italy, 2003/2006), Boon Manakitivipart (California, 4 visits), Horst Krekeler (Heidelberg Germany, 2 visits), Colin Lewis (Maine, published author), Arthur Skolnik, Walter Pall (Germany, European bonsai pioneer), Andy Smith/Golden Arrow Bonsai (South Dakota), Roy Nagatoshi (California, Karate Kid films), Min Hsuan Lo (Taiwan, 2 visits), Ted Matson (California Shohin Society founder), Bill Valavanis (International Bonsai magazine publisher), George LeBolt (New Jersey, Past President MABS), Tony Tickle (UK, BCI Board, 4 Ginkgo awards), Jim Barrett (California, Golden State Bonsai Federation), Sean Smith (Pennsylvania, Custom Oriental Woodcraft), Pedro Morales (Puerto Rico, 2 visits), Julian Adams (Scots Pine specialist), Charles Ceronio (South Africa), Michel Phaneuf (Canada), Michele Andolfo (Italy), David Knittle (Vermont, collected bonsai specialist), Kathy Shaner (California, 2 visits), Yasuo Mitsuya (Japan), Steve Tolley (UK), Chase Rosade (Pennsylvania, 'Mount Rushmore of American Bonsai Pioneers'), Peter Warren (UK, 2 visits), Isao Omachi (Japan), John Thompson, Minoru Akiyama (Japan, youngest Prime Minister's Award winner), Hirojoshi Yamaji (Japan, 2013 advanced BYOT workshop), David Easterbrook (Canada, Montreal Botanical Garden curator, 7 visits), Koji Hiramatsu (Japan, pine specialist), Ron Lang (potter), Suthin Sukosolvisit (Thailand/USA, Royal Bonsai Garden), Philip Shimko (USA, 2015), Budi Sulistyo (Indonesia, MABS 2015, Tropical Bonsai Gallery author), Bjorn Bjornholm (Tennessee, trained 6 years in Japan under Fujikawa), Mauro Stemberger (Italy), Marc Noelanders (Belgium, President European Bonsai Association), Will Baddeley (UK, deadwood specialist), Jonas Dupuich (California, Bonsai Tonight)

## Newsletter Archive

170+ issues available for free download spanning 2005-2022 — a treasure trove of meeting recaps, monthly care tips, guest artist features, workshop reports, member tributes, collecting trip stories, and club history. Written primarily by John Michalski.

## Monthly Bonsai Care Guide

Interactive month-by-month care guide for the Northeast US climate, distilled from 17 years of newsletters and decades of member experience. Covers pruning, repotting, watering, fertilizing, pest control, winter protection, and species-specific tips for deciduous, coniferous, and tropical bonsai.

## Club Traditions

- Holiday Party & Auction (trees, pots, tools, books)
- Summer Picnic Series at members' homes across northern NJ
- MABS Penny Vote for exhibition tree selection
- Copper Wire Drive (scrap metal recycling)
- Wild Collecting (Yamadori) trips — Maine, Nova Scotia, Morris County parks
- Community outreach at Cherry Blossom Festival, Earth Day, Bloomfest, Plant Sales, library lectures

## Presidential History

Mark Schmuck (~2005-2007), John Michalski (2007-2009, 2018-~2022), Diego Pablos (2009-2018), Diego Garnica (2024-present)
